source.title: Basal Ganglia Beta Oscillations Accompany Cue Utilization

confidence_rationale: Directly measures beta oscillations throughout cortical-BG circuits during a cued choice task in rats. Finds beta power elevated post-decision, after cue determines motor output -- consistent with a stabilized gating state that reduces interference from competing actions. This is the empirical referent for MECH-090 and MECH-057a: high beta = closed BG gate = committed-sequence protection. The framing as 'postdecision stabilized state' maps directly to MECH-057a's claim that committed action sequences suppress sensory precision reweighting. Confidence reduced slightly because the paper does not directly measure sensory precision or prediction-error suppression -- it establishes the beta correlate of commitment, not the downstream sensory effect.
failure_signatures: Beta power tracked movement suppression rather than post-commitment stabilization -- contradicts MECH-057a
Beta reset after cue was sufficient to gate action without any sustained elevation -- would weaken the 'committed state suppresses precision' framing
